title: Broken Queue: Incorrect Dequeue Operation on Front

statement.markdown: Implement a queue data structure with enqueue and dequeue operations. Enqueue should add an element to the end of the queue, while dequeue should remove the element from the front of the queue. If the queue is empty, raise an exception. The queue should handle zero-sized input and support multiple enqueues and dequeues.
statement.inputFormat: n integers separated by space, representing the queue elements
statement.notes: The queue should be implemented as a linked list, and the dequeue operation should remove the node from the front of the queue.

examples.explanation: Initial queue elements: 1, 2, 3. Dequeue operation removes the front element 1.
Empty queue. Dequeue operation raises an exception.

hints.text: Implementing the queue using a linked list will make it easier to add and remove elements from the front and back.
The dequeue operation should remove the node from the front of the queue, not the back.

editorial.title: Queue Implementation with Dequeue Operation
editorial.intuition: The queue can be implemented using a linked list. The enqueue operation adds a new node to the end of the list, while the dequeue operation removes the node from the front of the list.
editorial.bruteForce: Create a linked list node for each queue element and add it to the end of the list. When dequeue is called, remove the node from the front of the list.
editorial.betterSolution: Implement the queue using a circular doubly linked list to simplify the enqueue and dequeue operations.
editorial.optimalSolution: Create a circular doubly linked list node for each queue element. Add the new element to the end of the list and update the tail pointer. When dequeue is called, remove the node from the front of the list and update the head pointer.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The queue operations should be implemented correctly without any logical errors.
editorial.dryRun: Initial queue elements: 1, 2, 3. Enqueue operation adds a new element 4 to the back of the queue. Dequeue operation removes the front element 1.
commonMistakes: Removing the wrong node from the queue
Not updating the tail pointer after enqueue
